name: Massa Sovada (Portuguese Sweetbread)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
2. Dissolve yeast in warm water.
3. Scald milk and add it to the mixture of sugar, butter, and salt; stir until butter is melted.
4. Mix cinnamon with flour.
5. Add half the flour to the milk/sugar/butter/salt mixture, and mix until smooth.
6. Beat 3 eggs and add them along with the yeast to the mixture.
7. Continue adding remaining flour to form a soft dough. Remove from bowl and place on a floured surface.
8. Knead for about 15 minutes or until smooth and satiny.
9. Shape into a ball and place in a buttered bowl. Cover and let rise until doubled in size (2-1/2 to 3 hours).
10. Punch down the risen dough, divide it into two portions, and shape each portion into rounds.
11. Place shaped rounds in greased pans (8 inches round). Let rise again until doubled in size (1-1/2 to 2 hours).
12. Brush tops of dough with remaining beaten egg.
13. Bake in a preheated oven at 350 degrees Fahrenheit for 20-30 minutes or until golden brown.
14. Cool on wire racks.
